subject: Geology
title: Landslides and Fires
description: A quick, practical guide to Landslides and Fires.

hook.text: The 1906 San Francisco earthquake killed people, but the fires it sparked destroyed 80% of the city.

buildup.text: Earthquakes trigger secondary hazards: landslides, fires from ruptured gas lines, and dam failures.

discovery.text: Landslides from shaking bury roads and communities. In 1970, a Peruvian quake-triggered landslide killed 20,000.

twist.text: Secondary hazards often cause more destruction than the shaking itself. The quake is just the first domino.

climax.text: Earthquake preparedness must include secondary hazards. Gas shutoffs, fire response, and slope maps save lives.

punchline.text: The quake starts it; fire and slides finish it.

quiz.question: What destroyed most of San Francisco in 1906?
